message:
feat(extensions,security): add per-extension resource quota engine, least-privilege FS defaults, and improved denial diagnostics

SEC-4.1 / bd-b1d7o: Per-Extension Resource Quota Engine
---------------------------------------------------------
Add a configurable per-extension resource quota system that enforces
rate limits and cumulative budget caps at the hostcall dispatch chokepoint,
before the runtime risk evaluation layer.

New types:
- `ExtensionQuotaConfig`: configurable limits (hostcalls/s, hostcalls/min,
  total hostcalls, max subprocesses, max write bytes, max HTTP requests)
  with sensible defaults (100/s burst, 2000/min sustained, 8 subprocess cap)
- `ExtensionQuotaState`: mutable per-extension counters with sliding-window
  timestamps (VecDeque) for rate checks and monotonic counters for budgets
- `QuotaCheckResult`: Allowed | Exceeded { reason } enum for clear outcomes

The quota check integrates into `dispatch_host_call_shared()` after policy
resolution but before runtime risk scoring. Exceeded quotas produce a
structured `quota_exceeded` error code with a descriptive reason string
and are logged at WARN level with full extension/capability context.

ExtensionManager gains quota lifecycle methods:
- `check_quota()`: main dispatch-path check
- `record_subprocess_spawn/exit()`: track active subprocess fan-out
- `record_write_bytes()`: track cumulative write budget consumption
- `quota_state()`: inspection for telemetry/debugging
- `set_quota_config()`: runtime reconfiguration

The `ExtensionOverride` struct gains an optional `quota` field for
per-extension quota overrides (inherits global defaults when None).
All existing construction sites (extension_dispatcher, extension_preflight,
capability_policy_model, e2e_workflow_preflight tests) updated with
`quota: None` to match the new struct shape.

Least-Privilege Filesystem Defaults
------------------------------------
- Add `FsScopes::least_privilege_for_cwd()`: returns read-only project
  access (no write roots) as the default for extensions without a
  capability manifest. Previously `for_cwd()` granted both read and
  write access by default.
- `FsScopes::from_manifest()` now falls back to `least_privilege_for_cwd()`
  instead of `for_cwd()` when no manifest is present.
- Wasm host constructor (`HostState::new`) uses `least_privilege_for_cwd()`
  and enables `enforce_allowlist: true` on its HTTP connector by default.

Improved Denial Diagnostics
----------------------------
- FS connector: "No allowed roots configured" and "Path outside allowed
  scope" errors now include `hint` fields pointing users to declare
  `capability_manifest scope.paths`.
- HTTP connector: allowlist denial message now suggests declaring
  `capability_manifest scope.hosts`.
- Wasm host: removed unnecessary borrow in `eq_ignore_ascii_case` and
  `resolve_policy_decision` calls (clippy needless_borrows_for_generic_args).

Tests
-----
- `wasm_host_fs_defaults_to_read_only_without_manifest`: verifies that
  reads succeed and writes are denied under the new least-privilege default,
  with assertion on scope guidance in the error message.
- 16 quantile selection semantics tests (bd-xqipg): empty vec, single
  element, q=0/1 boundaries, odd/even counts, duplicates, NaN/infinity
  clamping, monotonicity, large sample, conformal residual integration.
- 4 clamp01 edge-case tests (bd-xqipg): NaN→0, -Inf→0, +Inf→1,
  normal passthrough.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
